growth_records.strain: subsp. multiplex CFBP 8418 (genome-scale model strain); the experimental growth rate value is cited from Leite et al. (ref 19) and is not strain-attributed in this paper
growth_records.medium: Xylem-sap-mimicking minimal medium with L-glutamine as sole carbon source (FBA condition 'close to experimental'); experimental growth rate originates from optimized synthetic medium in Leite et al. ref 19

up.method: arbitrary generic uptake constraint used in FBA to avoid bias (Materials and Methods), NOT an experimental measurement

mfa.note: FBA-PREDICTED (not measured). Fig 1C simulation on glutamine; growth/production rates were 'calculated from previous publications' (refs 19, 82). Majority of glutamine uptake converted to glutamate then alpha-ketoglutarate.
FBA-PREDICTED. Pyruvate generated from oxaloacetate via oxaloacetate decarboxylase.
FBA-PREDICTED. Pyruvate generated from malate via malic enzyme; remaining carbon wasted as CO2 (more than half through TCA cycle).

growth_records.cite: Gerlin L et al. Genome-Scale Investigation of the Metabolic Determinants Generating Bacterial Fastidious Growth. mSystems (2020). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00698-19. [PMC7112962]
growth_records.method: reported (cited from ref 19, Leite et al.), converted day^-1 -> h^-1
growth_records.snippet: we estimated that the experimentally observed X. fastidiosa growth rate (0.1608 day-1) (19) was recovered in silico with a 3.37-fold reduction of the optimal FBPase flux ... This reduction converted the generation time from 1.45 h (typical
growth_records.notes: This is primarily a genome-scale metabolic modeling (FBA) paper on the fastidious plant pathogen Xylella fastidiosa (model strain subsp. multiplex CFBP 8418). The ONLY quantitative experimental prokaryote growth-physiology value is a growth rate of 0.1608 day^-1, explicitly stated as 'experimentally observed' but CITED FROM reference 19 (Leite B, Andersen PC, Ishida ML) and NOT measured in this study. Conversion: mu = 0.1608 day^-1 / 24 h/day = 0.0067 h^-1; doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0067 = 103.4 h, consistent with the paper's stated generation time of 103 h (fastidious). No experimental substrate uptake or product secretion rates are measured in this paper. The glutamine uptake of 1 mmol/gDW/h is an ARBITRARY generic modeling constraint (stated in Methods: 'a generic L-glutamine uptake rate of 1 mmol.h-1.g(DW)-1 was used' to avoid bias), NOT experimental -> is_experimental:false. The mfa_fluxes listed are FBA-PREDICTED carbon splits (95%/22%/34% of carbon uptake for glutamine->glutamate, oxaloacetate decarboxylase, malic enzyme respectively) from the Fig 1C simulation whose flux inputs/outputs were 'calculated from previous publications' (refs 19, 82: Leite et al.; Navarrete F, De La Fuente L), so they are NOT measured fluxes. In-paper experimental work was qualitative Biolog Phenotype MicroArray carbon-source utilization (Table 2), scored via respiration rate with a 0.125 h^-1 threshold (relatively fast '++' vs slow '+'); no per-substrate numeric respiration values are in the article XML (they reside in supplemental Data Set S3). Reported: generation time can vary 10-100 h across strains; ~100 h doubling for some strains. EPS production predicted to cost >25% of growth rate (comparison to R. solanacearum). Recorded as one condition despite weak experimental content because the growth rate is the only extractable experimental physiology datum.
